metadata.emotional_engine: Fear of being laughed at for getting something wrong
metadata.curiosity_hook: Will Sami manage to hide his fear before everyone notices?

story_pages.text_arabic: فِي صَبَاحِ يَوْمِ الثُّلَاثَاءِ، وَقَفَ سَامِي فِي سَاحَةِ الْمَدْرَسَةِ وَنَظَرَ إِلَى الشَّبَكَةِ الْجَدِيدَةِ.
story_pages.text_english_literal: On Tuesday morning, Sami stood in the school playground and looked at the new net.
story_pages.illustration_note: Wide shot of the school playground. Sami stands alone near a volleyball net, hood half-up, looking uncertain. Other kids play in the background.

story_pages.text_arabic: قَالَ أُسْتَاذُ نَبِيلٌ بِصَوْتِهِ الْعَمِيقِ: «الْيَوْمَ، سَنَلْعَبُ الْكُرَةَ الطَّائِرَةَ.»
story_pages.text_english_literal: Ustadh Nabil said in his deep voice: 'Today, we will play volleyball.'
story_pages.illustration_note: Ustadh Nabil towering over the children, his owl features formal but not unkind. Children look excited. Sami's eyes are wide.

story_pages.text_arabic: فَرِحَ الْجَمِيعُ. صَفَّقَتْ لَيَانُ. قَفَزَ طَارِقٌ. لَكِنَّ سَامِي سَحَبَ غِطَاءَ رَأْسِهِ قَلِيلًا.
story_pages.text_english_literal: Everyone cheered. Layan clapped. Tariq jumped. But Sami pulled his hood down a little.
story_pages.illustration_note: Split reaction: Layan clapping with competitive fire, Tariq mid-jump with arms up, and Sami in the corner pulling his red hood lower.

story_pages.text_arabic: سَامِي لَمْ يَلْعَبِ الْكُرَةَ الطَّائِرَةَ مِنْ قَبْلُ. مَاذَا لَوْ ضَرَبَ الْكُرَةَ وَطَارَتْ فِي الاتِّجَاهِ الْخَطَأِ؟ مَاذَا لَوْ ضَحِكَ الْجَمِيعُ؟
story_pages.text_english_literal: Sami had never played volleyball before. What if he hit the ball and it flew the wrong way? What if everyone laughed?
story_pages.illustration_note: Close-up on Sami's worried face, thought bubbles showing a volleyball flying wildly while kids point and laugh.

story_pages.text_arabic: جَاءَ طَارِقٌ رَاكِضًا. «سَامِي! أَنَا وَأَنْتَ فِي فَرِيقٍ وَاحِدٍ! سَنَسْحَقُهُمْ!»
story_pages.text_english_literal: Tariq came running. 'Sami! You and I are on the same team! We'll crush them!'
story_pages.illustration_note: Tariq rushing toward Sami with his backwards green cap, arm around Sami's shoulder, big grin. Sami manages a weak smile.

story_pages.text_arabic: أَرَادَ سَامِي أَنْ يَقُولَ: «أَنَا لَا أَعْرِفُ كَيْفَ أَلْعَبُ.» لَكِنَّ الْكَلِمَاتِ عَلِقَتْ فِي حَلْقِهِ مِثْلَ حَبَّةِ حَلْوَى كَبِيرَةٍ.
story_pages.text_english_literal: Sami wanted to say: 'I don't know how to play.' But the words stuck in his throat like a big piece of candy.
story_pages.illustration_note: Sami with mouth slightly open, hand on throat, looking conflicted. Tariq already running toward the net.

story_pages.text_arabic: بَدَأَتِ الْمُبَارَاةُ. طَارَتِ الْكُرَةُ فَوْقَ الشَّبَكَةِ. ضَرَبَتْهَا لَيَانُ بِقُوَّةٍ. ضَرَبَهَا طَارِقٌ بِرَأْسِهِ — وَهَذَا لَيْسَ مِنَ الْقَوَاعِدِ أَصْلًا.
story_pages.text_english_literal: The game started. The ball flew over the net. Layan hit it hard. Tariq hit it with his head — which isn't even in the rules.
story_pages.illustration_note: Action scene: volleyball mid-air, Layan in a powerful stance, Tariq headbutting the ball with a goofy expression. Other kids laughing.

story_pages.text_arabic: وَسَامِي؟ وَقَفَ فِي الْخَلْفِ، يَتَمَنَّى أَنْ تَكُونَ الْكُرَةُ مَشْغُولَةً بِالْآخَرِينَ إِلَى الْأَبَدِ.
story_pages.text_english_literal: And Sami? He stood in the back, wishing the ball would stay busy with the others forever.
story_pages.illustration_note: Sami standing far back on his side of the court, small figure against the playground. Everyone else is engaged with the ball.

story_pages.text_arabic: ثُمَّ حَدَثَ مَا كَانَ يَخَافُ مِنْهُ. صَاحَ طَارِقٌ: «سَامِي! الْكُرَةُ لَكَ!»
story_pages.text_english_literal: Then the thing he feared happened. Tariq shouted: 'Sami! The ball is yours!'
story_pages.illustration_note: The volleyball arcing through the air directly toward Sami. Tariq pointing. All eyes turning to Sami.

story_pages.text_arabic: نَظَرَ سَامِي إِلَى الْكُرَةِ. نَظَرَتِ الْكُرَةُ إِلَى سَامِي. عَلَى الْأَقَلِّ هَذَا مَا شَعَرَ بِهِ.
story_pages.text_english_literal: Sami looked at the ball. The ball looked at Sami. At least that's what it felt like.
story_pages.illustration_note: Dramatic close-up: Sami and the volleyball facing each other, the ball almost cartoon-like in its intensity. A moment of frozen tension.

story_pages.text_arabic: ضَرَبَ الْكُرَةَ بِكِلْتَا يَدَيْهِ. طَارَتِ الْكُرَةُ... إِلَى الْأَعْلَى. عَالِيًا جِدًّا. ثُمَّ سَقَطَتْ... عَلَى رَأْسِهِ.
story_pages.text_english_literal: He hit the ball with both hands. The ball flew... upward. Very high. Then it fell... on his head.
story_pages.illustration_note: Comic sequence: Sami hitting the ball straight up, then the ball bonking him on the head. His hood falls back.

story_pages.text_arabic: ضَحِكَ بَعْضُ الْأَطْفَالِ. شَعَرَ سَامِي أَنَّ وَجْهَهُ أَصْبَحَ سَاخِنًا مِثْلَ مِقْلَاةِ بَابَا كَرِيمٍ.
story_pages.text_english_literal: Some children laughed. Sami felt his face become hot like Baba Karim's frying pan.
story_pages.illustration_note: Sami's face bright red, steam almost visible. A few kids giggling in the background. Sami's hood is down, leaving him exposed.

story_pages.text_arabic: قَالَتْ لَيَانُ: «لَازِمْ تَضْرِبُهَا لِلْأَمَامِ، مُو لِلسَّقْفِ!» لَمْ تَكُنْ تَقْصِدُ أَنْ تَكُونَ قَاسِيَةً. لَكِنَّ الْكَلِمَاتِ لَسَعَتْ.
story_pages.text_english_literal: 'You have to hit it forward, not at the ceiling!' Layan said. She didn't mean to be harsh. But the words stung.
story_pages.illustration_note: Layan gesturing forward with her hands, matter-of-fact expression. Sami flinching slightly at her directness.

story_pages.text_arabic: فَكَّرَ سَامِي: «سَأُصْلِحُ الْأَمْرَ. سَأَضْرِبُ الْكُرَةَ الْقَادِمَةَ بِقُوَّةٍ كَبِيرَةٍ حَتَّى يَنْسَى الْجَمِيعُ.»
story_pages.text_english_literal: Sami thought: 'I'll fix this. I'll hit the next ball so hard that everyone forgets.'
story_pages.illustration_note: Sami with a determined face, fists clenched. A thought bubble showing him spiking the ball heroically.

story_pages.text_arabic: جَاءَتِ الْكُرَةُ مَرَّةً أُخْرَى. رَكَضَ سَامِي. قَفَزَ. ضَرَبَ الْكُرَةَ بِكُلِّ قُوَّتِهِ.
story_pages.text_english_literal: The ball came again. Sami ran. Jumped. Hit the ball with all his strength.
story_pages.illustration_note: Dynamic action shot of Sami mid-leap, arms swinging at the volleyball. Intense effort on his face.

story_pages.text_arabic: طَارَتِ الْكُرَةُ فَوْقَ الشَّبَكَةِ... وَفَوْقَ الْمَلْعَبِ... وَفَوْقَ السُّورِ... وَاخْتَفَتْ فِي الشَّارِعِ.
story_pages.text_english_literal: The ball flew over the net... and over the court... and over the wall... and disappeared into the street.
story_pages.illustration_note: Wide shot tracking the volleyball's arc: over the net, over the playground wall, disappearing into the street beyond. Kids staring in shock.

story_pages.text_arabic: صَمَتَ الْجَمِيعُ. حَتَّى طَارِقٌ لَمْ يَجِدْ مَا يَقُولُهُ.
story_pages.text_english_literal: Everyone went silent. Even Tariq couldn't find anything to say.
story_pages.illustration_note: Frozen tableau: all the children standing still, mouths open. Tariq's cap slightly askew. Total silence.

story_pages.text_arabic: أَرَادَ سَامِي أَنْ يَخْتَفِيَ. أَرَادَ أَنْ يَكُونَ صَغِيرًا جِدًّا مِثْلَ نَمْلَةٍ وَيَنْزَلِقَ تَحْتَ السُّورِ.
story_pages.text_english_literal: Sami wanted to disappear. He wanted to be very small like an ant and slip under the wall.
story_pages.illustration_note: Sami shrinking, looking at the ground. A tiny imagined version of himself as an ant near the wall base.

story_pages.text_arabic: ثُمَّ سَمِعَ صَوْتًا. كَانَ طَارِقٌ... يَضْحَكُ. لَيْسَ ضَحِكًا عَلَيْهِ. بَلْ ضَحِكًا مُنْدَهِشًا.
story_pages.text_english_literal: Then he heard a sound. It was Tariq... laughing. Not laughing at him. But laughing in amazement.
story_pages.illustration_note: Tariq with genuine amazement on his face, mouth wide open in a laugh of wonder, not mockery.

story_pages.text_arabic: «يَا سَامِي! هَلْ رَأَيْتُمْ أَيْنَ وَصَلَتِ الْكُرَةُ؟! هَذِهِ أَقْوَى ضَرْبَةٍ فِي تَارِيخِ الْمَدْرَسَةِ!»
story_pages.text_english_literal: 'Sami! Did you all see where the ball went?! That's the strongest hit in school history!'
story_pages.illustration_note: Tariq pointing toward the wall where the ball disappeared, turning to the other kids with theatrical excitement.

story_pages.text_arabic: ضَحِكَتْ لَيَانُ. «صَحِيحٌ، لَكِنَّهَا فِي الاتِّجَاهِ الْغَلَطِ!» ثُمَّ ابْتَسَمَتْ. «لَكِنْ... الْقُوَّةُ مَوْجُودَةٌ.»
story_pages.text_english_literal: Layan laughed. 'True, but it went the wrong way!' Then she smiled. 'But... the power is there.'
story_pages.illustration_note: Layan trying to stay serious but breaking into a smile. Her competitive eyes show a glint of respect.

story_pages.text_arabic: جَلَسَ سَامِي عَلَى الْحَائِطِ الْمُنْخَفِضِ. لَمْ يَكُنْ مُسْتَعِدًّا لِلضَّحِكِ بَعْدُ. كَانَتْ أُذُنَاهُ لَا تَزَالَانِ سَاخِنَتَيْنِ.
story_pages.text_english_literal: Sami sat on the low wall. He wasn't ready to laugh yet. His ears were still hot.
story_pages.illustration_note: Sami sitting on the snack wall alone, head slightly down. The other kids are visible but at a distance, resuming play.

story_pages.text_arabic: جَاءَ طَارِقٌ وَجَلَسَ بِجَانِبِهِ. لَمْ يَقُلْ شَيْئًا فِي الْبِدَايَةِ. فَقَطْ أَخْرَجَ بِسْكُوِيتَةً مِنْ جَيْبِهِ وَقَسَمَهَا نِصْفَيْنِ.
story_pages.text_english_literal: Tariq came and sat next to him. He didn't say anything at first. He just took a cookie from his pocket and broke it in half.
story_pages.illustration_note: Intimate moment: Tariq and Sami on the low wall, Tariq breaking a cookie in half. Quiet, warm, no words needed.

story_pages.text_arabic: قَالَ طَارِقٌ بِفَمِهِ الْمُمْتَلِئِ: «أَتَعْرِفُ، أَوَّلَ مَرَّةٍ لَعِبْتُ كُرَةَ قَدَمٍ، رَكَلْتُ الْكُرَةَ فِي الْمَرْمَى الْغَلَطِ.»
story_pages.text_english_literal: Tariq said with his full mouth: 'You know, the first time I played soccer, I kicked the ball into the wrong goal.'
story_pages.illustration_note: Close-up of Tariq talking with crumbs on his mouth, gesturing casually. Sami listening, the tension in his shoulders starting to ease.

story_pages.text_arabic: نَظَرَ إِلَيْهِ سَامِي. «حَقًّا؟» قَالَ طَارِقٌ: «حَقًّا. وَالْجَمِيعُ صَفَّقُوا... لِلْفَرِيقِ الْآخَرِ.»
story_pages.text_english_literal: Sami looked at him. 'Really?' Tariq said: 'Really. And everyone clapped... for the other team.'
story_pages.illustration_note: Sami turning to look at Tariq with surprise. Tariq grinning sheepishly at the memory.

story_pages.text_arabic: ضَحِكَ سَامِي. ضَحْكَةً صَغِيرَةً هَادِئَةً. ثُمَّ ضَحْكَةً أَكْبَرَ.
story_pages.text_english_literal: Sami laughed. A small quiet laugh. Then a bigger one.
story_pages.illustration_note: Two-panel feel: first a tiny smile on Sami's face, then a genuine laugh. His hood is still down and he doesn't mind.

story_pages.text_arabic: قَالَ سَامِي: «لَكِنَّنِي فِعْلًا لَا أَعْرِفُ كَيْفَ أَلْعَبُ.» قَالَهَا أَخِيرًا. وَكَانَتْ أَسْهَلَ مِمَّا تَوَقَّعَ.
story_pages.text_english_literal: Sami said: 'But I really don't know how to play.' He finally said it. And it was easier than he expected.
story_pages.illustration_note: Sami looking relieved, shoulders down, speaking openly to Tariq. The weight visibly lifted.

story_pages.text_arabic: قَفَزَ طَارِقٌ. «مُمْتَازٌ! أَنَا سَأُعَلِّمُكَ! خُطْوَةٌ وَاحِدَةٌ: اضْرِبِ الْكُرَةَ لِلْأَمَامِ وَلَيْسَ لِلْفَضَاءِ الْخَارِجِيِّ!»
story_pages.text_english_literal: Tariq jumped up. 'Excellent! I'll teach you! Step one: hit the ball forward, not into outer space!'
story_pages.illustration_note: Tariq on his feet, coach-like pose with one finger up, oversized sneakers planted wide. Sami grinning.

story_pages.text_arabic: عَادَا إِلَى الْمَلْعَبِ. ضَرَبَ سَامِي الْكُرَةَ. لَمْ تَطِرْ بَعِيدًا. لَمْ تَكُنْ مِثَالِيَّةً. لَكِنَّهَا عَبَرَتِ الشَّبَكَةَ.
story_pages.text_english_literal: They went back to the court. Sami hit the ball. It didn't fly far. It wasn't perfect. But it crossed the net.
story_pages.illustration_note: The volleyball just barely clearing the net. Not a spectacular hit. Sami watching it with a small, satisfied expression.

story_pages.text_arabic: صَفَّقَ طَارِقٌ كَأَنَّ سَامِي فَازَ بِكَأْسِ الْعَالَمِ. ابْتَسَمَتْ لَيَانُ وَقَالَتْ: «أَحْسَنْتَ. الْمَرَّةُ الْقَادِمَةُ، أَقْوَى.»
story_pages.text_english_literal: Tariq clapped as if Sami had won the World Cup. Layan smiled and said: 'Good job. Next time, stronger.'
story_pages.illustration_note: Tariq applauding wildly, Layan giving a thumbs up with her competitive smile. Other kids resuming play naturally.

story_pages.text_arabic: فِي طَرِيقِ الْعَوْدَةِ إِلَى الْبَيْتِ، سَأَلَ سَامِي طَارِقًا: «هَلْ فِعْلًا رَكَلْتَ الْكُرَةَ فِي الْمَرْمَى الْغَلَطِ؟»
story_pages.text_english_literal: On the way home, Sami asked Tariq: 'Did you really kick the ball into the wrong goal?'
story_pages.illustration_note: The two friends walking home through the narrow Zuqaq alley, dappled light, relaxed posture.

story_pages.text_arabic: ضَحِكَ طَارِقٌ. «لَا. أَنَا اخْتَرَعْتُ الْقِصَّةَ.» ثُمَّ فَكَّرَ. «لَكِنْ كَانَ مُمْكِنًا يَصِيرُ.»
story_pages.text_english_literal: Tariq laughed. 'No. I made the story up.' Then he thought. 'But it could have happened.'
story_pages.illustration_note: Tariq laughing mischievously, hand behind his head. Sami shaking his head but smiling warmly.

story_pages.text_arabic: ضَحِكَ سَامِي وَرَفَعَ غِطَاءَ رَأْسِهِ قَلِيلًا. لَيْسَ لِأَنَّهُ خَائِفٌ. بَلْ لِأَنَّ الشَّمْسَ كَانَتْ فِي عَيْنَيْهِ.
story_pages.text_english_literal: Sami laughed and pulled his hood up a little. Not because he was scared. But because the sun was in his eyes.
story_pages.illustration_note: Final warm image: Sami walking with hood casually up, squinting at the golden sunset, smiling. Tariq beside him. The olive neighborhood glowing.

story_pages.text_arabic: بَدَأ كُلُّ شَيءٍ عِندَ الجِدارِ المُنخَفِضِ في ساحَةِ المَدرَسَة.
story_pages.text_english_literal: Everything started at the low wall in the school playground.
story_pages.illustration_note: A wide shot of the sun-baked playground with the low wall and a large oak tree in the background.

story_pages.text_arabic: حَيثُ تُتَبادَلُ الأسرارُ والوُعودُ الصَّغيرَة.
story_pages.text_english_literal: Where secrets and small promises are exchanged.
story_pages.illustration_note: A few other background children are whispering or trading snacks near the wall to establish the setting's routine.

story_pages.text_arabic: جاءَت نورة تَجري، شَعرُها مُبَعثَر والأرنَبُ «بَن بَن» تَحتَ ذِراعِها.
story_pages.text_english_literal: Noura came running, her hair messy and the rabbit 'Bun-Bun' under her arm.
story_pages.illustration_note: Noura (calico cat in a yellow polka-dot dress) looking energetic and determined, holding her stuffed rabbit.

story_pages.text_arabic: قالَت بِحَزم: «سامي، غَداً عِندي عَرضٌ أمامَ الصَّفّ. أُريدُكَ أن تَسمَعَني وَأنا أتَدَرَّب. وَعد؟»
story_pages.text_english_literal: She said firmly: 'Sami, tomorrow I have a presentation before the class. I want you to listen to me while I practice. Promise?'
story_pages.illustration_note: Noura looking up at Sami with a very serious expression, pointing at him while holding Bun-Bun.

story_pages.text_arabic:
نَظَرَ سامي إلى عَينَيها الواسِعَتَين. لَم يَستَطِع أن يَقولَ لا.
فَقال: «وَعد.»
story_pages.text_english_literal: Sami looked into her wide eyes. He couldn't say no. So he said: 'Promise.'
story_pages.illustration_note: Sami (orange cat in a red hoodie) smiling softly at his little sister, nodding his head.

story_pages.text_arabic: لَم تَمضِ خَمسُ دَقائِقَ حَتّى ظَهَرَ طارِق وَهوَ يَلهَث، قُبَّعَتُهُ الخَضراءُ مَقلوبَة.
story_pages.text_english_literal: Not five minutes passed until Tariq appeared panting, his green hat backwards.
story_pages.illustration_note: Tariq (beagle dog) running toward Sami, looking distressed and out of breath.

story_pages.text_arabic: قالَ بِصَوتٍ مَكسور: «سامي! بِطاقاتي ضاعَت! أظُنُّها سَقَطَت قُربَ شَجَرَةِ البَلّوط. ساعِدني غَداً بَعدَ الغَداء؟»
story_pages.text_english_literal: He said with a broken voice: 'Sami! My cards are lost! I think they fell near the oak tree. Help me tomorrow after lunch?'
story_pages.illustration_note: Tariq gesturing toward the large tree, looking very sad about his lost trading cards.

story_pages.text_arabic: بَلَعَ سامي ريقَهُ. بَعدَ الغَداءِ غَداً… نَفسُ الوَقتِ تَماماً.
story_pages.text_english_literal: Sami swallowed hard. Tomorrow after lunch... exactly the same time.
story_pages.illustration_note: Close-up on Sami’s face showing his 'what if' anxiety; his eyes are darting nervously.

story_pages.text_arabic:
لَكِنَّ طارِقاً كانَ يَنظُرُ إلَيهِ بِعَينَينِ مُتَوَسِّلَتَين.
فَسَمِعَ سامي نَفسَهُ يَقول: «طَبعاً… وَعد.»
story_pages.text_english_literal: But Tariq was looking at him with pleading eyes. So Sami heard himself say: 'Of course... promise.'
story_pages.illustration_note: Sami looking caught between his two friends, giving a hesitant thumbs-up to Tariq.

story_pages.text_arabic: في تِلكَ اللَّيلَة، جَلَسَ سامي على سَريرِهِ يُفَكِّر. وَعدان. وَقتٌ واحِد.
story_pages.text_english_literal: That night, Sami sat on his bed thinking. Two promises. One time.
story_pages.illustration_note: Sami in his room at night, sitting on his bed with his knees pulled up, looking worried.

story_pages.text_arabic: لَكِنَّهُ أقنَعَ نَفسَهُ: «سَأَبدَأُ مَعَ نورة، ثُمَّ أَركُضُ إلى طارِق، ثُمَّ أَعود. لَن يُلاحِظَ أحَد.»
story_pages.text_english_literal: But he convinced himself: 'I'll start with Noura, then run to Tariq, then return. No one will notice.'
story_pages.illustration_note: A thought bubble above Sami showing a little cartoon version of him running back and forth between Noura and Tariq.

story_pages.text_arabic: سَحَبَ غِطاءَ السَّريرِ فَوقَ رَأسِهِ، لَكِنَّ مَعِدَتَهُ كانَت تُؤلِمُهُ قَليلاً.
story_pages.text_english_literal: He pulled the bedsheet over his head, but his stomach hurt a little.
story_pages.illustration_note: Sami hiding under his red blanket, only his ears and eyes peeking out, looking uneasy.

story_pages.text_arabic: في اليَومِ التّالي، وَقَفَت نورة في زاوِيَةِ السّاحَةِ تَحمِلُ بَن بَن كَأنَّهُ مِيكروفون.
story_pages.text_english_literal: The next day, Noura stood in the corner of the yard holding Bun-Bun like a microphone.
story_pages.illustration_note: Noura standing on a small wooden crate, holding the stuffed rabbit to her mouth as if preparing for a big speech.

story_pages.text_arabic: قالَت: «اِبدَأ! اِسمَع!» بَدَأَت تَتَكَلَّمُ عَن أرنَبِها بِجِدِّيَّةٍ شَديدَة.
story_pages.text_english_literal: She said: 'Start! Listen!' She began to talk about her rabbit with great seriousness.
story_pages.illustration_note: Noura gesturing theatrically while Sami stands in front of her, trying to look attentive.

story_pages.text_arabic: أَومَأَ سامي بِسُرعَة، لَكِنَّ عَينَيهِ كانَتا تَنظُرانِ نَحوَ شَجَرَةِ البَلّوطِ البَعيدَة.
story_pages.text_english_literal: Sami nodded quickly, but his eyes were looking toward the distant oak tree.
story_pages.illustration_note: Split view: Sami nodding at Noura, but his eyes are fixed on Tariq far away across the playground.

story_pages.text_arabic:
قالَ فَجأة: «أحسَنتِ! اِستَمِرّي… سَأَعودُ بَعدَ لَحظَة!»
وَرَكَضَ سَريعاً.
story_pages.text_english_literal: He said suddenly: 'Well done! Continue... I'll be back in a moment!' and he ran fast.
story_pages.illustration_note: Sami sprinting away while Noura is mid-sentence, looking surprised and confused.

story_pages.text_arabic: عِندَ الشَّجَرَة، كانَ طارِق يَزحَفُ على رُكبَتَيهِ يُفَتِّشُ بَينَ الأوراقِ اليابِسَة.
story_pages.text_english_literal: At the tree, Tariq was crawling on his knees searching among the dry leaves.
story_pages.illustration_note: Tariq with his face close to the ground, surrounded by orange and brown leaves.

story_pages.text_arabic: قالَ بِحَماس: «أخيراً! تَعال، أظُنُّ واحِدَةً تَحتَ تِلكَ الصَّخرَة!»
story_pages.text_english_literal: He said excitedly: 'Finally! Come, I think one is under that rock!'
story_pages.illustration_note: Tariq pointing to a large grey rock; Sami joins him, looking over his shoulder nervously.

story_pages.text_arabic: نَظَرَ سامي إلى ساعَةِ المَدرَسَةِ القَديمَة. مَرَّت سِتُّ دَقائِق!
story_pages.text_english_literal: Sami looked at the old school clock. Six minutes had passed!
story_pages.illustration_note: A close-up of the school clock on the wall, and Sami's wide-eyed, panicked reaction.

story_pages.text_arabic: قالَ بِتَلَعثُم: «أنا… أحتاجُ ماءً! سَأَعود!» وَرَكَضَ مِن جَديد.
story_pages.text_english_literal: He said stuttering: 'I... I need water! I'll be back!' and he ran again.
story_pages.illustration_note: Sami running away from Tariq, who looks up from the ground with a puzzled expression.

story_pages.text_arabic:
عادَ إلى نورة فَوَجَدَها واقِفَةً وَيَداها على خَصرِها.
سَأَلَت: «أينَ ذَهَبت؟»
story_pages.text_english_literal: He returned to Noura and found her standing with her hands on her hips. She asked: 'Where did you go?'
story_pages.illustration_note: Noura looking grumpy and suspicious, staring at a breathless Sami.

story_pages.text_arabic:
قالَ وَهوَ يَلهَث: «كُنتُ… كُنتُ أَشرَبُ ماء.»
ضَيَّقَت نورة عَينَيها.
story_pages.text_english_literal: He said panting: 'I was... I was drinking water.' Noura narrowed her eyes.
story_pages.illustration_note: Noura leaning forward, squinting at Sami as if she's investigating his story.

story_pages.text_arabic: رَكَضَ مَرَّةً ثالِثَة إلى طارِق. هذِهِ المَرَّة وَصَلَ وَرُكبَتاهُ تَرتَجِفان.
story_pages.text_english_literal: He ran a third time to Tariq. This time he arrived with his knees trembling.
story_pages.illustration_note: Sami looking visibly exhausted, leaning against the oak tree trunk to catch his breath.

story_pages.text_arabic:
قالَ طارِق: «لِماذا تَنظُرُ إلى السّاعَةِ كُلَّ دَقيقَة؟»
لَم يَجِد سامي كَلِمَة.
story_pages.text_english_literal: Tariq said: 'Why do you look at the clock every minute?' Sami couldn't find a word.
story_pages.illustration_note: Tariq standing up, looking at Sami with concern and confusion.

story_pages.text_arabic:
رَكَضَ عائِداً لِنورة. كانَت تَعُدُّ ثَوانيَ غِيابِهِ بِصَوتٍ عالٍ:
«…اثنانِ وَأربَعون…»
story_pages.text_english_literal: He ran back to Noura. She was counting the seconds of his absence out loud: '...forty-two...'
story_pages.illustration_note: Noura counting on her fingers, looking very annoyed, while Bun-Bun is tucked firmly under her arm.

story_pages.text_arabic:
ثُمَّ حَدَثَ ما كانَ يَخافُهُ.
ظَهَرَت نورة فَجأةً خَلفَ شَجَرَةِ البَلّوط!
story_pages.text_english_literal: Then, what he feared happened. Noura suddenly appeared behind the oak tree!
story_pages.illustration_note: Noura has followed Sami; she pops out from behind the tree while Tariq and Sami are looking at the ground.

story_pages.text_arabic:
قالَت: «أَنتَ هُنا! مَعَ طارِق!»
سَألَ طارِق: «اِنتَظِر… كُنتَ مَعَ نورة أيضاً؟»
story_pages.text_english_literal: She said: 'You are here! With Tariq!' Tariq asked: 'Wait... you were with Noura too?'
story_pages.illustration_note: Sami standing frozen between Noura (on the left) and Tariq (on the right). Both look disappointed.

story_pages.text_arabic: مَشى سامي بِبُطءٍ نَحوَ الجِدارِ المُنخَفِض وَجَلَسَ عَلَيهِ وَحدَه.
story_pages.text_english_literal: Sami walked slowly toward the low wall and sat on it alone.
story_pages.illustration_note: Sami sitting on the wall, looking down at his feet. In the distance, Noura and Tariq are standing apart from him.

story_pages.text_arabic: حاوَلَ أن يَكونَ في مَكانَين، فَصارَ غائِباً عَنِ الاثنَين.
story_pages.text_english_literal: He tried to be in two places, so he ended up absent from both.
story_pages.illustration_note: A lonely shot of Sami. The playground seems quiet and the shadows are a bit longer.

story_pages.text_arabic: سَحَبَ قَلَنسُوَتَهُ الحَمراءَ فَوقَ رَأسِهِ. أحَسَّ بِشَيءٍ ثَقيلٍ في صَدرِه.
story_pages.text_english_literal: He pulled his red hood over his head. He felt something heavy in his chest.
story_pages.illustration_note: Sami with his hood up, hiding his face, showing he feels the shame of letting his friends down.

story_pages.text_arabic: أَنزَلَ القَلَنسُوَةَ وَقال: «أنا وَعَدتُكُما الاثنَين مَعاً. كُنتُ خائِفاً أن أُزعِلَكُما.»
story_pages.text_english_literal: He lowered the hood and said: 'I promised both of you together. I was afraid to upset you.'
story_pages.illustration_note: Sami standing up and walking toward his friends, looking them in the eye honestly.

story_pages.text_arabic: «ماذا لَو نورة تَتَدَرَّبُ هُنا، وَنَحنُ نُفَتِّشُ عَنِ البِطاقاتِ وَنَسمَعُها؟»
story_pages.text_english_literal: 'What if Noura practices here, while we search for the cards and listen to her?'
story_pages.illustration_note: Sami gesturing to the space around the tree, proposing his new idea to both of them.

story_pages.text_arabic: نورة تَتَكَلَّم، وَطارِق يُصَفِّق، وَسامي يَمشي بَينَهُما مُبتَسِماً.
story_pages.text_english_literal: Noura speaks, Tariq claps, and Sami walks between them smiling.
story_pages.illustration_note: A warm final scene: Noura on her crate speaking, Tariq finding a card and clapping, and Sami standing happily in the middle.

story_pages.text_arabic: طوال الأسبوع، كان طارق يحمل في جيبه كيسًا صغيرًا من القماش فيه قطع نقدية.
story_pages.text_english_literal: All week long, Tariq carried in his pocket a small cloth bag with coins in it.
story_pages.illustration_note: Tariq (the beagle/Saluki mix) walking happily with a noticeable bulge in his pocket.

story_pages.text_arabic: كلّما مشى، سمع صوت "طق طق طق" خفيفًا.
story_pages.text_english_literal: Whenever he walked, he heard a light 'tikk tikk tikk' sound.
story_pages.illustration_note: Close-up of Tariq's leg and pocket, with small musical notes or 'tikk tikk' text coming from the cloth bag.

story_pages.text_arabic: كان يبتسم كلّ مرة. هذه النقود لتيتا.
story_pages.text_english_literal: He smiled every time. This money was for Grandma (Teta).
story_pages.illustration_note: Tariq with a big, proud smile on his face, thinking of his Grandma.

story_pages.text_arabic: يوم الجمعة ستزورهم، وطارق يريد أن يشتري لها شيئًا جميلًا من بقالة عمّو حسّان.
story_pages.text_english_literal: On Friday she will visit them, and Tariq wants to buy her something beautiful from Uncle Hassan's grocery store.
story_pages.illustration_note: A thought bubble above Tariq's head showing a warm image of his Grandma and the colorful shop.

story_pages.text_arabic: لكن عند باب المدرسة، وقف ثلاثة أولاد يتكلّمون بصوت عالٍ.
story_pages.text_english_literal: But at the school door, three boys stood talking loudly.
story_pages.illustration_note: The school playground. Three older-looking children huddled together, gesturing excitedly.

story_pages.text_arabic:
— جرّبتوا رقائق البطولة الجديدة؟
— أحسن شي أكلته في حياتي!
story_pages.text_english_literal:
— Did you try the new Championship Chips?
— The best thing I've eaten in my life!
story_pages.illustration_note: The boys are very animated; one is pretending to crunch on something delicious.

story_pages.text_arabic: — الكيس الأخير كان عند عمّو حسّان... مش عارف إذا بقي شي.
story_pages.text_english_literal: — The last bag was at Uncle Hassan's... I don't know if any are left.
story_pages.illustration_note: One boy points toward the street where the shop is located, looking worried about the stock.

story_pages.text_arabic: نظر طارق إلى سامي. سامي رفع كتفيه ولم يقل شيئًا.
story_pages.text_english_literal: Tariq looked at Sami. Sami shrugged his shoulders and said nothing.
story_pages.illustration_note: Sami (the orange tabby) looks hesitant, his hood slightly up, while Tariq looks wide-eyed and alert.

story_pages.text_arabic: لكن طارق شعر بشيء غريب في بطنه، شيء يشبه الجوع لكنّه أسرع.
story_pages.text_english_literal: But Tariq felt something strange in his stomach, something like hunger but faster.
story_pages.illustration_note: Tariq holding his stomach, his expression shifting from happy to intense and focused.

story_pages.text_arabic: شيء يقول: "لازم تجرّب قبل ما تخلص!"
story_pages.text_english_literal: Something saying: 'You must try it before it's finished!'
story_pages.illustration_note: Tariq looking determined, his ears slightly forward, caught in the grip of a new 'want'.

story_pages.text_arabic: طول النهار، لم يستطع طارق أن يفكّر في شيء آخر. الكيس البرّاق. النكهة الخاصّة.
story_pages.text_english_literal: All day long, Tariq couldn't think of anything else. The shiny bag. The special flavor.
story_pages.illustration_note: Tariq sitting at his desk in class, drawing a shiny, golden potato chip bag on his notebook.

story_pages.text_arabic:
همس لسامي في الاستراحة:
— سامي، ماذا لو... ماذا لو ما نزلت هذه الرقائق مرّة ثانية أبدًا؟
story_pages.text_english_literal:
He whispered to Sami during recess:
— Sami, what if... what if these chips never come out again?
story_pages.illustration_note: Tariq and Sami by the playground wall. Tariq is leaning in urgently; Sami is listening with a concerned look.

story_pages.text_arabic:
سامي فكّر قليلًا ثمّ قال بهدوء:
— لكن... مصاري تيتا؟
story_pages.text_english_literal:
Sami thought for a bit then said quietly:
— But... Teta's money?
story_pages.illustration_note: Sami points to Tariq's pocket where the bag of coins is hidden.

story_pages.text_arabic:
هزّ طارق رأسه بسرعة.
— بلاقيلها شي ثاني. هذي فرصة العمر يا سامي. فرصة العمر!
story_pages.text_english_literal:
Tariq shook his head quickly.
— I'll find her something else. This is the chance of a lifetime, Sami. The chance of a lifetime!
story_pages.illustration_note: Tariq making a grand gesture with his hands, trying to convince himself as much as Sami.

story_pages.text_arabic: بعد المدرسة، دفع طارق باب بقالة عمّو حسّان. الجرس الصغير فوق الباب رنّ.
story_pages.text_english_literal: After school, Tariq pushed the door of Uncle Hassan's grocery. The small bell above the door rang.
story_pages.illustration_note: Entrance to the cozy shop. Tariq is entering with Sami trailing slightly behind him.

story_pages.text_arabic: عمّو حسّان، السلحفاة العجوز، رفع رأسه ببطء من خلف الطاولة ولم يقل شيئًا.
story_pages.text_english_literal: Uncle Hassan, the old tortoise, raised his head slowly from behind the counter and said nothing.
story_pages.illustration_note: Uncle Hassan behind his wooden counter, looking through his glasses with a calm, observant gaze.

story_pages.text_arabic: على الرفّ العلوي، كيس واحد فقط. كبير، لامع، مكتوب عليه بأحرف ذهبية: "رقائق البطولة!"
story_pages.text_english_literal: On the top shelf, only one bag. Big, shiny, written on it in golden letters: 'Championship Chips!'
story_pages.illustration_note: Low angle looking up at a very shiny, golden bag that seems to glow compared to the other snacks.

story_pages.text_arabic: أخرج طارق كيس القماش. فتحه. عدّ النقود مرّتين.
story_pages.text_english_literal: Tariq took out the cloth bag. He opened it. He counted the money twice.
story_pages.illustration_note: Tariq's paws carefully arranging the coins on the wooden counter; his brow is furrowed in concentration.

story_pages.text_arabic: نظر إلى الكيس اللامع، ثمّ إلى النقود، ثمّ إلى الكيس مرّة أخرى. وضع النقود على الطاولة.
story_pages.text_english_literal: He looked at the shiny bag, then at the money, then at the bag again. He put the money on the table.
story_pages.illustration_note: A moment of hesitation. Tariq's eyes are moving back and forth between the prize and his savings.

story_pages.text_arabic: عمّو حسّان نظر إلى النقود، ثمّ نظر إلى طارق. لكنّه لم يقل شيئًا. أعطاه الكيس ببطء.
story_pages.text_english_literal: Uncle Hassan looked at the money, then looked at Tariq. but he said nothing. He gave him the bag slowly.
story_pages.illustration_note: Uncle Hassan's wrinkled hand sliding the shiny bag toward Tariq while holding his gaze.

story_pages.text_arabic: جلس طارق وسامي على الرصيف. فتح طارق الكيس بحماس.
story_pages.text_english_literal: Tariq and Sami sat on the sidewalk. Tariq opened the bag with excitement.
story_pages.illustration_note: The two friends sitting outside the shop. A small 'puff' of air comes out as the bag opens.

story_pages.text_arabic: أوّل رقاقة... مقرمشة. مالحة. ثاني رقاقة... نفس الشيء.
story_pages.text_english_literal: The first chip... crunchy. Salty. The second chip... the same thing.
story_pages.illustration_note: Tariq eating with a big crunch. Sami watching him curiously.

story_pages.text_arabic:
توقّف طارق عن المضغ.
— سامي...
— نعم؟
— هل... هل طعمها عادي؟
story_pages.text_english_literal:
Tariq stopped chewing.
— Sami...
— Yes?
— Does it... does it taste ordinary?
story_pages.illustration_note: Tariq holds a half-eaten chip, looking disappointed. The excitement has left his face.

story_pages.text_arabic:
سامي أكل رقاقة أخرى ببطء وقال بصدق:
— طعمها مثل أيّ رقائق ثانية.
story_pages.text_english_literal:
Sami ate another chip slowly and said honestly:
— It tastes like any other chips.
story_pages.illustration_note: Sami chewing thoughtfully, looking at the bag which doesn't seem so special anymore.

story_pages.text_arabic: نظر طارق إلى الكيس اللامع بأحرفه الذهبية. الكيس كان جميلًا جدًّا. لكن ما في داخله كان... عاديًّا جدًّا.
story_pages.text_english_literal: Tariq looked at the shiny bag with its golden letters. The bag was very beautiful. But what was inside it was... very ordinary.
story_pages.illustration_note: Tariq holding the empty, crumpled shiny bag. He looks a little slumped.

story_pages.text_arabic:
سأل سامي بهدوء:
— وتيتا؟ ماذا ستشتري لها يوم الجمعة؟
story_pages.text_english_literal:
Sami asked quietly:
— And Teta? What will you buy for her on Friday?
story_pages.illustration_note: Sami looking at Tariq with soft eyes. Tariq is staring at his empty hands.

story_pages.text_arabic: لم يُجب طارق. ضمّ الكيس الفارغ بين يديه. الهواء خرج منه بصوت صغير حزين: "فسسس."
story_pages.text_english_literal: Tariq didn't answer. He squeezed the empty bag between his hands. The air came out with a small sad sound: 'Fssss.'
story_pages.illustration_note: Close up of the bag being crushed; the golden letters are wrinkled and the shine is gone.

story_pages.text_arabic: مرّت عربة بائع الورد في الشارع. وردة حمراء صغيرة تهتزّ فوق العربة. فجأة رفع طارق رأسه.
story_pages.text_english_literal: A flower seller's cart passed in the street. A small red rose was shaking atop the cart. Suddenly Tariq raised his head.
story_pages.illustration_note: A colorful cart with various flowers passing by. One single bright red rose stands out.

story_pages.text_arabic:
— سامي... كم معك؟
أخرج سامي من جيبه قطعتين صغيرتين.
— هذا كلّ شيء.
story_pages.text_english_literal:
— Sami... how much do you have?
Sami took two small pieces from his pocket.
— This is everything.
story_pages.illustration_note: Sami showing two small coins in his palm. Tariq is frantically searching his own pockets.

story_pages.text_arabic: وجدت طارق قطعة نقدية واحدة ناسيها في الجيب الخلفي. ركض الاثنان خلف عربة الورد.
story_pages.text_english_literal: Tariq found one coin he had forgotten in his back pocket. The two ran after the flower cart.
story_pages.illustration_note: Tariq and Sami running down the street, waving their arms to stop the flower seller.

story_pages.text_arabic:
نظر البائع إلى القطع في كفّ طارق. ابتسم.
— بالضبط هذا ثمنها.
story_pages.text_english_literal:
The seller looked at the coins in Tariq's palm. He smiled.
— That is exactly its price.
story_pages.illustration_note: The flower seller handing the single red rose to Tariq. The three coins are in the seller's hand.

story_pages.text_arabic: يوم الجمعة، فتحت تيتا الباب. طارق مدّ يده والوردة الحمراء فيها. ضمّته وقالت: "يا قلبي."
story_pages.text_english_literal: On Friday, Teta opened the door. Tariq held out his hand with the red rose in it. She hugged him and said: 'My heart.'
story_pages.illustration_note: A very warm scene at the doorway. A kind older cat (Teta) hugging Tariq tightly; the rose is visible in the foreground.

story_pages.text_arabic: في طريق العودة، مشى طارق بجانب سامي. الجيب فارغ، لكنّ شيئًا دافئًا كان لا يزال في صدره.
story_pages.text_english_literal: On the way back, Tariq walked beside Sami. The pocket was empty, but something warm was still in his chest.
story_pages.illustration_note: Tariq and Sami walking into the sunset. Tariq is patting his flat pocket, but he is smiling peacefully.
